meaning: n.同时(或同地)发生,同时存在,合作
examples: Any change ought to require the general concurrence of all concerned.
The concurrence of their disappearances had to be more than coincidental.
The doctor may seek the concurrence of a relative before carrying out the procedure.
The sharply worded concurrence in a decision by the influential U.S. Court of Appeals D.
Four justices in the Hamdan majority joined Stephen Breyer's concurrence, which expressly invited Congress to authorize military commissions.
